methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Type One Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Blykalla, a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Janne Wallenius and Peter Szakalos, has raised $60M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a sector universe of 11 peers—3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private companies including Type One Energy, TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy, and Oklo—though the narrow peer set limits the applicability of certain methods.

Stage-progression analysis, the primary applicable method, uses the $60M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to later-stage progression. This yields an implied valuation of $114M. A sensitivity range accounting for variance around this multiplier produces a low-end estimate of $97M and a high-end estimate of $131M. Peer-median valuation analysis was not applicable given insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the nuclear energy subsector; applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias.

The triangulated valuation range of $97M–$131M, with a median of $114M, rests primarily on stage-based progression rather than market comparable multiples. Key uncertainties include technology maturity relative to peers, capital efficiency relative to sector burn rates, and the timing and probability of near-term financing events that would anchor market comparables. Additional data on peer funding multiples and revenue/burn metrics would strengthen conviction in this range.
